title: Hereditary Angioedema Treatment Market Size, Drugs, and Outlook
text: The hereditary angioedema (HAE) therapeutics market size was $3.60 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach $8.18 billion by 2033, at a CAGR of 10.43% from 2026 to 2033. The HAE market is highly competitive with multiple recent drug approvals and pipeline candidates. In August 2025, Ionis Pharmaceuticals received FDA approval for Dawnzera, the first RNA-targeting therapy for the prophylactic treatment of HAE. This followed FDA approvals in July for KalVista Pharmaceuticals' Ekterly, the first on-demand pill for the condition, and in June for CSL's prophylactic factor XIIa inhibitor Andembry. Intellia's lonvo-z (NTLA-2002) is a CRISPR-based gene therapy aiming to prevent HAE attacks with a single dose and is currently in Phase 3 clinical trial. Major companies operating in the global hereditary angioedema treatment industry include CSL Behring, Shire, Pharming Group NV, BIOCRYST Pharmaceuticals, Ionis Pharmaceuticals, Attune Pharmaceuticals, Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals, and Adverum.
